---
title: "sign_flips"
generated: true
description: "Build a matrix of $ pm 1$ sign flips for permutation inference."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.permutation`](../nitrix-stats-inference-permutation.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:sign_flips -->
```python
sign_flips(n: int, n_perm: int, key: Array, *, blocks: Optional[Int[Array, 'N']] = None) -> Float[Array, 'n_perm N']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:sign_flips -->
Build a matrix of $\pm 1$ sign flips for permutation inference.

Each row of the returned `(n_perm, n)` matrix is one relabelling of the
`n` observations under sign-flip exchangeability, suitable for symmetric
or one-sample tests. Row 0 is always the all-ones identity (the unflipped
data). Without `blocks` each observation flips independently; with
`blocks` every observation sharing a block label shares that block's
sign, so the block flips as a whole.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:sign_flips -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `n` | `int` | — | — | Number of observations (columns of the output). |
| `n_perm` | `int` | — | — | Number of relabellings to generate, including the identity row. |
| `key` | `Array` | — | — | A `jax.random` key seeding the Bernoulli draws of the flip signs. |
| `blocks` | `Optional[Int[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | Per-observation integer exchangeability-block labels of shape `(n,)`. Observations sharing a label flip together. Labels may be arbitrary (negative or non-contiguous) integers; they are canonicalised to a dense encoding so each distinct label is one independent whole-block flip. If `None` (the default), every observation flips independently. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `n_perm` × `N`. A `(n_perm, n)` matrix whose entries are `+1.0` or `-1.0`. Row 0 is all ones (the identity); each remaining row is one sign-flip relabelling.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:sign_flips -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_perm` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
